title: A LLM-informed multi-agent AI system for drone-based visual inspection for infrastructure
abstract: This paper explores the application of LLM-powered agent systems in drone-based visual inspection, focusing on three core aspects. First, a multi-agent framework is proposed, composed of five specialized sub-agents—Router, PathPlanner, Controller, Perceptioner, and Retriever—that collaboratively handle inspection subtasks such as 3D spatial reasoning, path planning, and UAV control. Second, a novel image-based pipeline is introduced to generate multi-criteria semantic point clouds and abstract them into 3D Scene Graphs (3DSGs), enabling spatial-semantic reasoning aligned with human intent. These 3DSGs act as both knowledge storage and reasoning engines. Third, the system is evaluated through simulations and laboratory experiments, demonstrating its ability to automate inspection workflows and provide a foundation for extensible AI-agent systems.
methodology: The paper proposes a multi-agent AI framework for drone-based infrastructure visual inspection. The system consists of five LLM-based agents (Router, Retriever, PathPlanner, Controller, Perceptioner) implemented using LangChain and LangGraph. The Router uses the ReAct approach for dynamic task planning. The Retriever employs knowledge-graph-based RAG to query structural information from 3D Scene Graphs stored in Neo4j via Cypher queries. A novel image-based pipeline generates multi-criteria semantic point clouds (with structure-level, component-level, and damage-level labels) from 2D images using COLMAP for 3D reconstruction and semantic segmentation models (SemanticSAM, Mask-RCNN, color-based segmentation), which are then abstracted into 3DSGs. The PathPlanner generates coverage flight paths from point cloud data, the Controller executes drone commands, and the Perceptioner handles image understanding (detection, segmentation, captioning) via VLMs. The system supports two-level human-machine interaction during path planning and task review cycles. Experiments are conducted in both Unreal Engine/AirSim simulation environments (steel truss bridges) and a laboratory setting (wind turbine model with DJI Tello drone), with comparative evaluation across six LLMs on four task types of varying complexity.
results: GPT-4o achieved 100% success rate across all four requirement types (information retrieval, topological querying, inspection with path planning, and parameterized inspection), demonstrating the best overall performance. GPT-4o mini succeeded on Requirements 1 and 3 but failed on Requirements 2 and 4. Other models (GPT-3.5 Turbo, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Haiku, DeepSeek-V3) showed inconsistent performance, with common failure modes including incorrect Cypher query generation, inability to summarize retrieved information (causing infinite loops), and tool calling errors. In the laboratory experiment with a physical wind turbine model, GPT-4o successfully completed both information retrieval and drone-based inspection tasks. The 3DSG was successfully constructed for both simulated bridges (with multi-criteria semantic point clouds) and the physical wind turbine model (57 nodes, 286 edges), enabling effective spatial-semantic reasoning.
contribution: 首次将LLM驱动的多智能体AI系统应用于无人机基础设施视觉检测，提出包含五个专业智能体（Router、PathPlanner、Controller、Perceptioner、Retriever）的协作框架，并引入3D场景图（3DSG）作为LLM空间感知与推理的知识引擎，实现了从自然语言意图到自动化检测工作流的全链路集成
